skill.name: slash-command-encoder
skill.description: Designs slash command interfaces for AI skills, cascades, agents, and model routing. It helps create command names, parameters, validation rules, chaining patterns, and Claude Code command catalogs.
skill.summary: Design slash commands for AI skills and Claude Code workflows.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are documentation examples in SKILL.md rather than executable code. The main confirmed concern is that the skill instructs assistants to scan hidden Claude skill directories and generate Claude Code command files, which needs explicit user consent.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Claude Directory Discovery and Command Registration
medium_findings.description: The skill describes scanning ~/.claude/skills and .claude/skills, then generating command definitions under .claude/commands. This is legitimate for command catalog creation, but it touches hidden user configuration and should require user approval before reading or writing files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names hidden Claude skill paths and command generation paths. The intent appears functional, not malicious, but filesystem access to user configuration is a real risk surface.

low_findings.title: Shell-Style Command Examples Are Documentation, Not Executable Logic
low_findings.description: Many static command execution hits come from fenced examples showing slash command pipelines, chaining, and model routing. SKILL.md contains instructions and examples, not a script that directly executes shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious syntax appears inside markdown fences and slash-command examples. No executable script, backtick shell execution, or runtime wrapper was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at several lines, but review found no cryptographic algorithm implementation, hashing routine, encryption call, or key handling in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found ordinary documentation text and workflow examples at the cited locations. No evidence supports a real weak cryptography issue.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Flags Are Not Supported by Context
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system and network reconnaissance, but the cited content describes model selection, file input descriptions, and workflow examples. No host probing, port scanning, network enumeration, or data exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are examples or parameter descriptions. The review found no network probes, scanner commands, or unauthorized collection logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Chaining Patterns Could Trigger Side Effects if Implemented Directly
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents pipelines, conditional execution, and deployment-style slash commands. These are examples, but generated commands should be reviewed before they invoke build, deployment, web search, or model automation tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risk depends on downstream implementation. The current file is documentation, but the suggested command patterns could cause side effects when converted into real commands.

content.user_title: Create Slash Commands for AI Workflows
content.value_statement: Repeated AI workflows can become slow and inconsistent when users rely on long natural language prompts. This skill helps create clear slash commands with parameters, validation, routing, and chaining for Claude Code, Codex, and related tools.
actual_capabilities: Designs slash command names using verb-noun, agent, model, and workflow patterns.
Defines parameters, flags, validation rules, defaults, and help text for commands.
Maps commands to micro-skills, cascades, agents, and model-specific routes.
Shows chaining patterns for sequential, parallel, conditional, and multi-model workflows.
Describes discovery of installed skills and generation of Claude Code command catalog entries.
Provides templates for data, code, agent, multi-model, and workflow command families.
limitations: It is a design and documentation skill, not a standalone command runner.
Generated commands still need human review before they read, write, deploy, or call external tools.
Auto-discovery depends on access to local Claude skill directories.
Multi-model routing depends on the related tools being installed and configured.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team AI Shortcuts
use_cases.description: Create consistent slash commands for code review, testing, auditing, and refactoring workflows across a team.

use_cases.title: Build Personal Claude Code Commands
use_cases.description: Convert repeated prompts into concise commands with clear parameters and reusable help text.

use_cases.title: Plan Multi-Model Workflows
use_cases.description: Route tasks between Claude, Codex, Gemini, agents, and cascades using explicit command patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slash command for this repeated task: [describe task]. Include the command name, parameters, help text, and two examples.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a slash command for [workflow]. Add required parameters, optional flags, allowed values, defaults, and validation errors.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Command Family
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a command family for [domain]. Include naming rules, shared parameters, routing targets, and help text for each command.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Chained Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a chained slash command workflow for [goal]. Include phases, model routing, failure handling, output formats, and review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Create a command for validating API responses against a schema.
output_examples.output: A validate-api command specification with required file input, optional schema and strict flags, help text, routing target, and example usage.

output_examples.input: Create a command catalog for code quality workflows.
output: A lint command for style checks and optional fixes.
A test coverage command with report output.
A security analysis command routed to an audit workflow.

output_examples.input: Create a multi-model research and implementation workflow.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow that routes research, design, implementation, and audit steps to suitable AI tools with explicit review points.

best_practices: Ask for approval before scanning hidden Claude directories or writing command files.
Keep each command focused, named clearly, and documented with expected inputs and outputs.
Review generated commands before using them for deployment, file modification, or external tool calls.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad commands that hide destructive actions behind short names.
Do not route sensitive files to external tools without explicit user consent.
Do not skip parameter validation for file paths, model names, or output locations.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides command designs, templates, and workflow patterns. Execution depends on the environment that implements the commands.

faq.question: Can it create Claude Code command files?
faq.answer: It describes how to generate Claude Code command definitions. Users should approve any file writes before registration.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security warning?
faq.answer: The skill asks to scan hidden Claude skill directories and generate command files. That is useful but should be consent based.

faq.question: Does it support Codex workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates include Codex routing examples for prototyping, reasoning, and audit workflows.

faq.question: Can it route tasks to multiple models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for Claude, Codex, Gemini, agents, and cascades when those tools are available.

faq.question: What should I review before using generated commands?
faq.answer: Review file access, external tool calls, model routing, output paths, and any deployment or modification steps.
